summary: Implement a new 'Contact Us' page with a functional contact form using shadcn/ui components and integrate it into the application's routing.

steps.description: Create a new page component file for the Contact Page. This file will contain the UI and logic for the contact form.
steps.details: Create `ContactPage.tsx` inside the `client/src/pages/` directory. This component will render a form with fields for name, email, and message, along with a submit button. It will utilize shadcn/ui components like `Card`, `Input`, `Textarea`, `Button`, `Label`, and `Separator` for a consistent look and feel. Basic state management for form inputs will be included.

steps.description: Integrate the new Contact Page into the application's routing system.
steps.details: Modify the main application routing file, typically `client/src/App.tsx`, to add a new route. A new `<Route>` element will be added for the path `/contact` that renders the `ContactPage` component. This will make the contact page accessible via the `/contact` URL.

steps.description: Verify the new Contact Page is accessible and functional.
steps.details: Start the development server and navigate to `/contact`. Verify that the Contact Page renders correctly with all form fields and the submit button. Test submitting the form (though actual backend integration for submission is out of scope for this request, the client-side form handling should be tested).

risks: Potential conflict with existing routing configurations if `App.tsx` is heavily customized.
Styling conflicts if global CSS or other styling solutions are not compatible with shadcn/ui's default styling.
rollback_strategy: Revert the changes in `client/src/pages/ContactPage.tsx` and `client/src/App.tsx` to their previous versions. This can be done by discarding the new file and removing the added route configuration.
success_criteria: The new 'Contact Us' page is accessible at the `/contact` URL, displays a functional form with name, email, and message fields, and the form can be submitted (client-side console log confirmation is sufficient for this request).
